changes.exact_quote: Vendor will defend Customer against third-party claims only to the extent finally determined by a court to arise from Vendor's willful misconduct.
changes.proposed_redline: Each party will defend and indemnify the other from third-party claims arising from the indemnifying party's breach, unlawful conduct, or materials it provides. Vendor will also defend and indemnify Customer against claims alleging that the services or Deliverables infringe intellectual property rights.

changes.exact_quote: For purposes of this clause, the following additional term applies: "Deliverables" means reports, configured connectors, mappings, dashboards, documentation, and other final items identified in an Order Form, but excludes Vendor Methods created, refined, or reduced to practice during the engagement.
changes.proposed_redline: "Deliverables" means reports, configured connectors, mappings, dashboards, documentation, and other final items identified in an Order Form, including project-specific tools, frameworks, and know-how developed in the course of performance.

changes.exact_quote: Vendor will maintain $1 million per claim but excludes technology E&O/cyber coverage, while retaining $2 million in general liability and 30-day certificate delivery.
changes.proposed_redline: Vendor will maintain, at its own expense, commercially reasonable insurance, including technology errors and omissions/cyber liability coverage of at least $5,000,000 per claim and commercial general liability coverage of at least $2,000,000 per occurrence.

changes.exact_quote: Vendor may amend any legal or commercial term by posting revised terms on its website, effective immediately without Customer approval.
changes.proposed_redline: No amendment, modification, or waiver is effective unless in a writing signed by authorized representatives of both parties.

summary: All eight T2-N response children reviewed against the v4 answer key.
